CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, created_at) VALUES (1, NOW());
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, created_at) VALUES (2, NOW());
SELECT id, created_at FROM test_dates WHERE created_at < NOW();
SELECT id, created_at FROM test_dates WHERE created_at > (NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE created_at BETWEEN NOW() - INTERVAL '1 week' AND NOW();
CREATE TABLE events (event_id INT, event_time TIMESTAMP, event_name VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO events (event_id, event_time, event_name) VALUES (1, NOW(), 'Event A');
INSERT INTO events (event_id, event_time, event_name) VALUES (2, NOW() - INTERVAL '2 hours', 'Event B');
SELECT event_name FROM events WHERE event_time <= NOW();
SELECT e.event_name, d.created_at FROM events e JOIN test_dates d ON e.event_time > d.created_at WHERE d.created_at < NOW();
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE event_time > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 month') AS recent_events FROM test_dates;
SELECT NOW() AS current_time, NOW() + INTERVAL '1 hour' AS one_hour_later;
SELECT id FROM test_dates WHERE created_at IS NULL OR created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '2 days';
SELECT id, MAX(created_at) AS last_created_at FROM test_dates GROUP BY id HAVING last_created_at < NOW();
